content: Personality Disorders (PDs) are enduring patterns of inner experience and behavior deviating markedly from cultural expectations, pervasive across situations, inflexible, stable over time, leading to distress/impairment. Onset in adolescence/early adulthood. DSM-5 clusters: Cluster A (odd/eccentric)—Paranoid (distrust/suspiciousness), Schizoid (detachment, restricted affect), Schizotypal (acute discomfort with closeness, cognitive/perceptual distortions, eccentric behavior). Cluster B (dramatic/emotional/erratic)—Antisocial (disregard for others), Borderline (instability), Histrionic (attention-seeking), Narcissistic (grandiosity, need for admiration). Cluster C (anxious/fearful)—Avoidant (inhibition, inadequacy), Dependent (excessive need to be cared for), Obsessive-Compulsive PD (preoccupation with orderliness, perfectionism, control). Prevalence ~10-15% overall.
